data.title: U.S. Bancorp targets 200+ basis points operating leverage in 2025 amid strategic expense management
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: U.S. Bancorp (USB) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Gunjan Kedia highlighted Q1 EPS of $1.03 and emphasized the company's strategic focus on disciplined expense management, organic growth, and payments transformation. She affirmed confidence in medium-term targets despite economic uncertainties. Net charge-off ratio and capital levels showed improvements, with a CET1 capital ratio of 10.8% this quarter.
  • Gunjan Kedia outlined three strategic priorities for achieving goals: managing expenses sharply, driving organic growth across businesses, and transforming the payments division.
  • CFO John Stern noted the company achieved year-over-year positive operating leverage of 270 basis points. He pointed out a $10 million reserve release due to improved asset quality. He also stated the company’s tangible book value per share rose to $25.64 as of March 31.

Outlook

  • The company expects Q2 2025 net interest income between $4.1 billion and $4.2 billion and non-interest income of approximately $2.9 billion. Non-interest expenses are forecasted at $4.2 billion or lower. Management reiterated its full-year guidance for adjusted total revenue growth in the range of 3% to 5% and positive operating leverage exceeding 200 basis points for 2025.
  • Stern emphasized the company is carefully monitoring macroeconomic conditions, including tariff-related uncertainties, and believes its guidance is achievable under various scenarios.

Financial Results

  • Net interest income for Q1 2025 was $4.12 billion, stable compared to the prior quarter after adjusting for two fewer days. Non-interest income increased 5% year-over-year to $2.8 billion, supported by payments and trust and investment management fees, though impacted by seasonal declines and reduced trust fees.
  • Average deposits were $507 billion, a 1.1% decrease quarter-over-quarter, consistent with seasonal trends. Loan balances increased modestly to $379 billion, driven by commercial lending.
  • Non-interest expenses totaled $4.2 billion in Q1 2025, consistent with Q4 2024 levels, reflecting strong expense control and operational efficiencies.
  • Asset quality improved, with the net charge-off ratio at 0.59% and non-performing assets at 0.45% of loans and other assets. The allowance for credit losses stood at $7.9 billion, or 2.07% of loans.

Q&A

  • Gerard Cassidy, RBC Capital Markets, asked about the ideal interest rate environment. CFO John Stern explained the company prefers a more upward-sloping curve, which benefits funding positions and fixed asset repricing.
  • Scott Siefers, Piper Sandler, inquired about the fee growth trajectory. Stern confirmed mid-single-digit growth expectations, with confidence in payment services, trust, and capital markets segments.
  • Mike Mayo, Wells Fargo, questioned CEO Gunjan Kedia on how her leadership will differ from her predecessor. Kedia emphasized a renewed focus on expense discipline, organic growth capabilities, and urgency in execution.
  • Erika Najarian, UBS, sought clarification on the pace of share repurchases. Stern noted the company plans to increase buybacks as the CET1 ratio approaches 10% under Category II rules, balancing capital accretion with distributions.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst sentiment was neutral to slightly positive, with questions focusing on interest rate impacts, fee growth drivers, and share repurchase pacing. Analysts maintained a cautious tone regarding macroeconomic uncertainties and the payments business.
  • Management exhibited confidence in achieving guidance, emphasizing disciplined expense management and growth initiatives. Kedia's comments reflected a focus on restoring investor confidence through consistent execution.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Positive operating leverage guidance for Q1 2025 exceeded Q4 2024's 190 basis points with a Q1 result of 270 basis points. Loan and deposit growth remained modest but aligned with expectations.
  • Management's tone remained optimistic, emphasizing progress in strategic priorities and improved financial metrics compared to the prior quarter.
  • Analysts' focus shifted slightly toward concerns about payments business performance and the impact of broader macroeconomic conditions on guidance.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged macroeconomic uncertainties, including tariff-related risks, as potential challenges. They highlighted ongoing efforts in expense flexibility and risk management to navigate these uncertainties.
  • Analysts voiced concerns regarding the payments business, particularly in achieving growth targets amid competitive pressures.

Final Takeaway

U.S. Bancorp emphasized its commitment to delivering 200+ basis points of positive operating leverage for 2025, supported by disciplined expense management and organic growth initiatives. Management is confident in its ability to navigate macroeconomic uncertainties while focusing on payments transformation and capital efficiency. The company’s improved asset quality and stable financial performance bolster its strategic direction.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Keystone pipeline restarts as South Bow finishes leak repairs
data.description:
South Bow (NYSE:SOBO) said Wednesday it has safely restarted the Keystone pipeline after receiving regulatory approval from the Pipeline and Hazardous Materials Safety Administration, a week after a rupture caused an estimated 3,500 barrels of oil to spill in North Dakota.

Most of the oil that was released has been recovered and remediation efforts have started, South Bow (NYSE:SOBO) said, adding it will continue to monitor the system as an investigation continues into the cause of the spill.

South Bow (SOBO) restarted the Keystone pipeline at a reduced pressure on April 14, delayed by inclement weather and coming two days after it met all conditions imposed by the PHMSA, which also said the failed section of the 30-inch OD pipe will be transported to a metallurgical lab in Houston for testing.

The agency said Keystone will be allowed to operate at no more of 80% of pressure levels at the time of the April 8 spill; at the time of the failure, the line was transporting 17,844 barrels per hour, or the equivalent of 428K bbl/day.

Keystone can transport as much as 620K bbl/day of Canadian crude to U.S. Midwest and Gulf Coast markets.

data.title: ASML targets €30B-€35B in 2025 revenue amid strong AI-driven semiconductor demand
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: ASML Holding N.V. (ASML) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Christophe Fouquet highlighted strong first-quarter financial results, with total net sales of €7.7 billion, aligned with guidance, and a gross margin of 54%, exceeding expectations. He emphasized the growing demand for AI-related semiconductor technologies as a key driver of growth for the company.
  • CFO Roger Dassen provided details on the revenue breakdown, with net system sales at €5.7 billion, including €3.2 billion from EUV and €2.5 billion from non-EUV systems. Installed Base Management sales contributed €2 billion. He also noted a free cash flow of -€475 million, attributed to customer payment dynamics and investments in fixed assets.
  • Fouquet discussed progress in EUV technologies, such as the NXE:3800E reaching full specification and supporting high-volume manufacturing. Additionally, milestones in High NA systems were noted, with customers achieving significant performance improvements and process simplifications.
  • Dassen mentioned ongoing challenges such as increased uncertainty regarding tariffs, which could impact the business environment, though mitigation strategies are in place.

Outlook

  • The company reiterated its 2025 revenue guidance of €30 billion to €35 billion, with a gross margin expectation of 51%-53%. The full-year growth is expected to be supported by Logic and Memory demand, with AI-related applications being a leading growth driver.
  • Q2 2025 guidance projects net sales between €7.2 billion and €7.7 billion and a gross margin of 50%-53%. Installed Base Management sales are anticipated to remain steady at around €2 billion.
  • Management expressed confidence in 2026 being a growth year but acknowledged uncertainties due to macroeconomic conditions and tariff-related complexities.

Financial Results

  • Q1 2025 earnings per share were reported at €6. Net income stood at €2.4 billion, representing 30.4% of total net sales.
  • Gross margin exceeded expectations due to favorable EUV product mix and enriched configurations. Operating expenses were in line with guidance, with R&D expenses at €1.161 billion and SG&A expenses at €281 million.
  • The order book reflected net system bookings of €3.9 billion, with Logic accounting for 60% and Memory for 40%.

Q&A

  • Francois-Xavier Bouvignies, UBS, inquired about High NA adoption timelines and flexibility in pricing. CEO Fouquet explained that tool maturity remains a priority for adoption over pricing flexibility, with efforts focused on achieving readiness for volume manufacturing.
  • Krish Sankar, TD Cowen, asked about growth trajectories and the role of bookings in sustaining growth. CFO Dassen noted that while bookings for 2026 need to materialize, the backlog provides a solid base for continued growth.
  • Analysts raised concerns regarding the impact of tariffs on business operations. Management clarified that discussions with customers have not yet led to changes in business plans, and efforts are ongoing to minimize tariff-related burdens across the value chain.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts exhibited a neutral to slightly positive tone, with focused questions on growth opportunities, High NA adoption, and tariff impacts.
  • Management maintained a confident tone during prepared remarks but demonstrated cautious optimism when discussing geopolitical uncertainties and tariff implications.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone seemed more cautious due to heightened macroeconomic uncertainties.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue decreased from €9.3 billion in Q4 2024 to €7.7 billion in Q1 2025, reflecting seasonality and the absence of High NA revenue recognition.
  • Gross margin improved from 51.7% in Q4 2024 to 54% in Q1 2025 due to favorable product mix.
  • Installed Base Management sales remained steady, while free cash flow turned negative due to increased investments and customer payment dynamics.

Risks and Concerns

  • Tariff-related uncertainties remain a key challenge, with potential direct and indirect impacts on ASML, its customers, and suppliers.
  • Management highlighted uncertainties in global economic conditions that could affect customer demand and operational costs.
  • Analysts expressed concerns about potential delays in High NA adoption and changes in customer spending behavior.

Final Takeaway

ASML's Q1 2025 results reinforced its position as a leader in semiconductor manufacturing technology, driven by AI-related demand and advancements in EUV and High NA platforms. While management expressed confidence in achieving 2025 revenue guidance, uncertainties related to tariffs and macroeconomic conditions warrant close monitoring.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Netflix Q1 earnings on deck: What to expect
data.description:
Netflix (NASDAQ:NFLX) is set to post earnings on Thursday, with the quarter becoming the first for the streaming giant to not disclose its subscriber count, a metric that is traditionally looked as a key performance indicator by investors.

Wall Street expects the California-based company to post EPS of $5.68, while revenue is expected to rise 12% to $10.51 billion during the quarter.

Last year, Netflix announced its decision to not report subscribers numbers and urged investors to focus on revenue and operating margins to assess progress. Earlier in January, the company logged a record number of subscribers over the holiday quarter, leading to the stock soaring over 10% in after-hours trading.

Netflix already demonstrated its dominance in the streaming market through its content slate, which includes a mix of originals, live sporting events, popular series and movies. The company also built its success with the help of its important ad-supported service tier.

The streamer announced an Extra Member with Ads offering to expand its footprint in 10 of its 12 "ads countries."

“While massive subscriber growth was the primary driver in 2024, we expect price increases to drive revenue growth in 2025, and the ad tier to drive revenue higher in 2026,” said Wedbush analyst Alicia Reese, adding that the introduction of the extra member feature for the ad tier is providing another revenue growth opportunity.

Analysts also expect the company to demonstrate resilience in a weaker global macroeconomic situation. Oppenheimer’s Jason Helfstein said Netflix remains the “cleanest story” in its coverage and reiterated Outperform rating, given no trade exposure or material impacts from a U.S. or global recession.

A recent report, saying Netflix is aiming to hit a $1 trillion market capitalization and a doubling of its revenue by 2030, also encouraged investors.

Seeking Alpha analysts and Wall Street are both positive and rated the stock Buy, while Seeking Alpha’s Quant ratings consider it Hold.

Over the last three months, EPS estimates have seen two upward revisions, compared to 17 downward revisions, while revenue estimates have been revised upwards seven times versus 12 downward moves.

The company’s stock gained nearly 9% so far this year, outperforming the over 9% fall in the broader S&P500 Index.

data.title: SA analyst upgrades: AMZN, AMD, BAC, TTD, IBKR, NOW, GEV, BC
data.description:
Here are the latest rating upgrades issued by Seeking Alpha analysts:

Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN). Hold to buy by Oliver Rodzianko. "My conservative price target of $280 per AMZN share over the next 12 months is dependent on no severe near-term volatility shocks. However, even if such shocks do occur, the strategic initiatives are in place for long-term success."

Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ:AMD). Hold to buy by Bay Area Ideas. "AMD's recent 20% decline presents a buy opportunity; technicals show mixed but improving signs, suggesting bullish reversal potential."

Bank of America (NYSE:BAC). Hold to buy by Oakoff Investments. "I'm upgrading Bank of America Corporation stock to "Buy" after Q1 2025 earnings beat expectations, showing strong EPS and revenue growth, and resilience in NII…BAC stock is trading at a significant discount to its historical P/E and peer group, presenting a potential 24.72% upside."

ServiceNow (NYSE:NOW). Hold to buy by Star Investments. "As the company continues to scale, its operating margin and FCF should continue expanding, eventually leading the market to value it more like Salesforce (CRM) or Microsoft (MSFT). Investors with long-term investment mindsets should consider putting the stock on their buy list."

Trade Desk (NASDAQ:TTD). Hold to buy by Julian Lin. "Despite competitive pressures and market volatility, TTD's strong balance sheet and reasonable valuation present a solid buying opportunity."

Trade Desk (NASDAQ:TTD). Hold to buy by Shareholders Unite. "Trade Desk faces temporary setbacks due to a bungled platform transition and sales reorganization, but remains a market leader in DSP with strong AI integration…While TTD stock is attractive at $50, economic uncertainties suggest caution; consider buying a first tranche but remain vigilant for further soft quarters given the worsening outlook for the economy."

Interactive Brokers (NASDAQ:IBKR). Sell to hold by Daniel Urbina. "Given the fairer valuation with the stock drop, I upgrade my rating from "sell" to "hold" despite mid-term headwinds in trading volume due to lower asset levels."

GE Vernova (NYSE:GEV) Sell to buy by Motti Sapir. "The stock looks mispriced given the company’s improving execution and long-term growth potential. A 20-30% upside is realistic as margins normalize, and investor sentiment catches up."

Brunswick (NYSE:BC). Sell to buy by Jeffrey Fischer. "With Brunswick being primarily a U.S. manufacturer whose clients are mostly in America, the direct tariff impacts here are fairly limited. Additionally, any continuation of U.S. dollar weakness would present a tailwind for the company."

data.title: McDonald's is bringing back its popular Snack Wrap menu item this year
data.description:
McDonald's (NYSE:MCD) plans to bring back its popular Snack Wrap menu item sometime in 2025, according to a cryptic social media post. The restaurant chain teased that the launch would be on the 14th, but did not specify which month. 

The Snack Wrap, which has been off the McDonald's (NYSE:MCD) menu for almost a decade, was first introduced in the United States in 2006 and quickly became a fan favorite due to its convenient, handheld format and lighter profile compared to traditional burgers. The original version featured either crispy or grilled chicken, shredded lettuce, cheese, and a choice of ranch or honey mustard sauce, all wrapped in a soft flour tortilla.  Over the last five years, various social media posts asking McDonald's (MCD) to bring back Snack Wrap have gone viral.

The portability and satisfying taste of Snack Wrap helped boost McDonald's (MCD) sales almost immediately. However, despite the popularity of Snack Wrap, the restaurant chain discontinued it in 2016 due mainly to operational inefficiencies. The assembly process was considered too time-consuming for the chain’s fast-paced kitchens, and the wrap ultimately didn’t meet internal sales expectations. 

The revived Snack Wrap is expected to feature some updates, aligning with contemporary tastes while preserving the qualities that made it a fan favorite in the first place

While the exact launch date remains under wraps for competitive reasons, the Chicago-based company has confirmed that the item will be back before the end of the year in what could be a noteworthy development for fast-food rivals such as Burger King (QSR), Taco Bell (YUM), KFC (YUM), Wendy's (WEN), Shake Shack (SHAK), and Jack in the Box (JACK) scrapping for market share.

data.title: SUNation Energy sinks after approving 200-for-1 reverse stock split
data.description:
SUNation Energy (NASDAQ:SUNE) -48.8% in Wednesday's trading after saying its board approved a 200-for-1 reverse stock split effective April 21, just six months after the seller of photovoltaic solar energy systems and battery storage systems executed a 50-for-1 reverse split.

SUNation Energy (NASDAQ:SUNE) said the reverse split is intended to allow it to regain compliance with the minimum bid price required for continued listing on the Nasdaq Capital Market, after shares have plunged 99% YTD.

A week ago, the company said it raised ~$20M in aggregate gross proceeds via a securities purchase agreement with certain institutional investors, helping eliminate $12.6M of secured debt and other long-term contractual obligations.

data.title: Fed's 'modestly restrictive' policy stance apt for taming inflation, Hammack says
data.description:
Federal Reserve Bank of Cleveland President Beth Hammack signaled Wednesday her support for maintaining the current "modestly restrictive" monetary policy stance to bring inflation down to the central bank's goal. 

Like many of her colleagues, Hammack wants to stay the course on the Fed's wait-and-see approach as high tariff uncertainty continues to cloud the economic outlook.

"With inflation elevated, the current modestly restrictive stance for monetary policy is appropriate to continue the downward pressure on inflation so that it returns to our goal in a timely fashion," she said in remarks prepared for delivery at a Columbus Metropolitan Club event in Columbus, Ohio.

However, should the economy stumble and inflation fall, then rates cuts may be appropriate, she said. Conversely, "monetary policy may need to follow a more restrictive trajectory" if the labor market stays healthy and inflation rises persistently.

A more stagflationary dynamic of higher inflation and a cooling labor market, meanwhile, would result in "some challenging tradeoffs" for monetary policy, she said. "In that case, it will be important to ensure inflation expectations remain well anchored while assessing the likely magnitude and persistence of the misses to each side of our dual mandate goals."

"I would rather be slow and move in the right direction than move quickly in the wrong direction," Hammack said. "Fortunately, I think we are in position to assess the incoming data, the risks to the outlook, and the appropriate policy response to achieve our longer-term objectives. If only the path to meet both parts of our mandate was as clear as following the yellow brick road."

data.title: Seeking Alpha’s top Quant picks in Energy sector as Q1 earnings loom
data.description:
The S&P 500's Energy sector (NYSEARCA:XLE), which holds a 4.2% weightage on the S&P 500 Index and includes companies that are into oil production, drilling, refining and transportation, gained nearly 8% during the first quarter, compared to over 4% decline in the broader market.

According to FactSet, the Energy sector is expected to report the largest (year-over-year) earnings decline of all eleven sectors at -13.7%. This is primarily due to lower oil prices, with Q1 2025 averaging $71.38, down 10% from Q1 2024’s $76.91.

At the sub-industry level, three of the five sub-industries in the sector are predicted to report a year-over-year decline in earnings: Oil & Gas Refining & Marketing (-103%), Integrated Oil & Gas (-13%), and Oil & Gas Equipment & Services (-9%), said the report.

On the other hand, two sub-industries are predicted to report year-over-year growth in earnings: Oil & Gas Storage & Transportation (13%) and Oil & Gas Exploration & Production (10%), it added.

For Q1 2025, the estimated Y/Y earnings growth rate for the S&P 500 is above 10%.

According to the latest data from Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating System, stocks making up the energy index have secured an average health score of 3.32. The system awards grades based on quantitative measures, like valuation, earnings growth and recent stock performance. The highest possible score for any individual company is a 5.

Looking at individual companies in the sector, based on their quant ratings, one out of 22 stocks is rated as a Strong Buy and two as a Buy, while 19 stocks are rated Neutral. None of the stocks are considered Sell or lower.

Exxon Mobil (XOM) is leading with the highest Quant rating of 4.76, with profitability and momentum factors boosting its ratings. The company, in January 2025, posted better than expected Q4 adjusted earnings, driven by high-margin projects in the Permian Basin and Guyana.

ConocoPhillips (COP) stands in the second spot with a score of 4.16 and a Buy rating.

Coterra Energy (CTRA), Expand Energy (EXE) and EQT Corporation (EQT) follow with Quant scores of 4.09, 3.46 and 3.41, respectively.

Halliburton (HAL) has the lowest Quant score of 2.87 in the sector. Shares of the U.S. oilfield services firm declined over 21% so far this year.

data.title: Tesla loses traction in California with registrations down for sixth straight quarter
data.description:
Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) registrations in California fell for the sixth consecutive quarter, dropping 15.1% year-over-year, even as overall new car registrations in the state rose 8.3%, according to Experian Automotive data.

“Tesla’s troubles continue to worsen as Californians are giving the cold shoulder to the direct-to-consumer automaker (and controversial owner, Elon Musk),” the California New Car Dealers Association said in response to the results.

With registrations down, Tesla’s market share was down by 11.6% at the end of Q1 to 43.9%, now holding less than half of the California Zero Emission Vehicle (“ZEV”) market for the year. This weighed on overall ZEV sales momentum, with a second quarterly decline in registrations at just 20.8% of the total market share, down from 22% a year ago.

“Dealers sell what customers want to buy. No mandate can force consumers to choose otherwise. Although the manufacturers we represent are increasing EV sales in California, with the substantial decline in Tesla sales, EV market penetration is largely flat. This puts us well short of EV sales mandates that take effect this year,” said Robb Hernandez, CNCDA’s Chairman.

For the full year, overall registrations are expected to drop 2.3% to 1.71M vehicles.

Among total California vehicle registrations, Toyota (TM) held the top spot with a 16.5% market share, Honda (HMC) took second with 10.8%, with Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) in third place at 9.1%.
